rows.snippet: Limiting Connections to WebLink or Web Access Through IIS. 19 years ago Limiting Connections to WebLink or Web Access Through IIS. September 11, 2006 | KB: 1011627 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011627/limiting-connections-to-weblink-or-web-access... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011627/limiting-connections-to-weblin ...
rows.title: Limiting Connections to WebLink or Web Access Through IIS.
rows.detailTitle: Limiting Connections to WebLink or Web Access Through IIS.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Limiting Connections to WebLink or Web Access Through IIS. Product: Web Access 6, WebLink 6, Web Access 7, WebLink 7 KB: 1011627 Published: September 11, 2006 Last Updated: September 11, 2006 Summary You can limit the simultaneous connections to WebLink or Web Access by changing the maximum connections IIS allows to the website that WebLink or Web Access runs under. Resolution You can configure this setting through the IIS Manager. To configure the maximum connections allowed to a website in IIS 6 Load the IIS Manager MMC snap-in. Expand the server item. Expand the Web Sites item. Select the website that controls your Web Access or WebLink virtual directory. By default, it should be "Default Web Site." From the Action menu, click Properties. Click the Performance tab. In the Web site connections section, select the Connections limited to option, then specify the desired maximum number of allowed simultaneous connections. To configure the maximum connections allowed to a website in IIS 5 (Windows 2000) Load the IIS Manager MMC snap-in. Expand the server item. Select "Default Web Site" From the Action menu, click Properties. On the Web Site tab, in the Connections section, select the Limited To option, then specify the desired maximum number of allowed simultaneous connections. Note: IIS 5 for Windows XP contains a built-in limit of 10 maximum connections. More Information Performing the above procedure throttles all connections to the specified IIS website. If you have more than one virtual directory configured under that website, those applications will also be affected by the connection limit. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Changing the Number of Context Hits Displayed on WebLink 8's Search Results Page. 15 years ago kb/1012775/changing-the-number-of-context-hits-displayed-on-weblink-8-39-s-search-results-page- WebLink 8 Summary When a WebLink user performs a full-text search, by ... Context H... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012775/changing-the-number-of-context-hi ...
rows.title: Changing the Number of Context Hits Displayed on WebLink 8's Search Results Page.
rows.detailTitle: Changing the Number of Context Hits Displayed on WebLink 8's Search Results Page.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Changing the Number of Context Hits Displayed on WebLink 8's Search Results Page. Product: WebLink 8 KB: 1012775 Published: April 4, 2011 Last Updated: April 5, 2011 Summary When a WebLink user performs a full-text search, by default, only the first five context hits will be displayed for each matching document. For example, if a document contains the word apple 15 times, by default the search results page will only show five context hits. You can modify this behavior. Changing the Number of Displayed Context Hits Complete the following steps: On the machine hosting WebLink, browse to WebLink's installation drive. In most cases, this will be C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 8. Browse into the Web Files folder, then the xsl folder. Open a file named SearchResults.xsl in a text editor (e.g., Notepad). Find the line that starts with <xsl:for-each select=''ContextHits/ContextResult Change the number at the end of this line to be one greater than the number of context hits you want displayed (it is 6 by default). For example, if you want 10 hits displayed, change this number to 11. Save and close the file. There is no need to restart any services or machines, as this change will take effect immediately. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: PDF Files Generated by WebLink use the Same Resolution as the Scanned Images.
rows.detailTitle: PDF Files Generated by WebLink use the Same Resolution as the Scanned Images.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith PDF Files Generated by WebLink use the Same Resolution as the Scanned Images. Product: WebLink 7 KB: 1011830 Published: June 11, 2007 Last Updated: June 11, 2007 Summary WebLink has the ability to export documents as PDF files. The resolution of the generated PDF files is determined by the resolution of the scanned images of the document. For example, images scanned at 300 DPI become 300 DPI PDF files. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Product: WebLink 7.0.2 KB: 1001001 Published: July 11, 2005 Last Updated: July 11, 2005 Summary This article documents the list of changes for WebLink 7.0.2. More Information See the following list of changes and fixes for version 7.0.2. Changes for Version 7.0.2 WebLink now includes language support for Spanish and Portuguese. (21476) Fixes for Version 7.0.2 When you add a password to the LoginInterface tag of the Login.aspx page, the Login.aspx page did not display the password when you browsed to the page. The page now displays the password as asterisks in the Password field. (21784) A "Culture name is not supported" or "Input string was not in correct format" error could occur when a web browser's language was set to a non-English language. This issue is now resolved. (21007) Related Links Please refer to the following article to learn more about Laserfiche WebLink 7.0.2. 1001002 Known Issues with WebLink 7.0.2. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3 8 years ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013902/release-notes-for-laserfiche-forms-10-3 Forms 10.3 Summary Laserfiche Forms is a Web application that allows organizations to collect, process, and rout... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013902/release-notes-for-laserfiche-form ...
rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3 Product: Forms 10.3 KB: 1013902 Published: December 6, 2017 Last Updated: December 6, 2017 Summary Laserfiche Forms is a Web application that allows organizations to collect, process, and route information. Users can create Web forms to collect or work with information, as well as business processes to define how that information is routed, interacted with, and managed. This major release of Forms includes a new inbox layout with a more extensive completed tasks list and resizable columns. The process designer now has a fixed right pane to let you select process objects without leaving the pane, a minimap to help you navigate larger processes, multi-select options that let you move and delete many objects, and error messages on the objects and in the properties pane to help you locate your errors. The redesigned reports section includes custom reports, which let you generate reports about many processes. For a more detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1013903. Laserfiche Forms Server System Requirements CPU: 4-core or more CPU Memory: 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) Operating system: Windows 7 Windows 8 Windows 8.1 Windows 10 Windows Server 2008 (SP 2+) Windows Server 2008 R2 Windows Server 2012 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2016 Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Laserfiche Forms server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. Note: Server operating systems (Windows Server 2008 R2, etc.) are preferred. Database Server Microsoft SQL Server 2008 Microsoft SQL Server 2008 R2 Microsoft SQL Server 2012 Microsoft SQL Server 2014 Microsoft SQL Server 2016 Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Laserfiche Forms Software Requirements Laserfiche Server Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Server (version 9 or later) or Laserfiche Directory Server. Forms is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). Web Server Internet Information Services (IIS): IIS 7 (Windows 7, Windows Server 2008) IIS 7.5 (Windows 7, Windows Server 2008 R2) IIS 8 (Windows 8, Windows Server 2012) IIS 8.5 (Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 R2) IIS 10 ASP.NET 4.x must be enabled. Client Workstation Users can fill out forms that start a process in Chrome (latest version), Safari(Mac only), Firefox (latest version), Internet Explorer 11 and later, Edge, Opera. The Laserfiche Forms Inbox, Form Designer, Process Designer, Administrative pages, etc. must be viewed in Internet Explorer 11, Edge, Firefox (latest version), or Chrome (latest version). Laserfiche Forms also supports mobile browsing (Chrome, Firefox, and Safari recommended). Known Issues You cannot resize images in the rich text editor using Edge and Chrome. To resize an image, use Firefox. (52631) In Edge and Internet Explorer, you will not see canvas dots when you zoom in and out several times. (54447) When you generate a table report sorted by Status in ascending order and then save a snapshot of the report, the snapshot has the incorrect ordering. (51907) If you use a lookup to append rows to a table or collection, and you insert an extra line in the field that contains existing data or read-only data, then the new data will not be saved. (57042) If a field is not read-only, but is in a read-only section, back-end validation will still occur. This may allow for invalid input that is only caught after a form is submitted. (54824). The address column in a report is removed when you upload an older version of the form to the current version of Forms. (27559) Access rights set for the operational dashboard are lost when you upgrade to Forms 10.3. This means that all user will have access to the operational dashboard. (37343) The Process Designer will no longer remember your most recent zoom and position settings, when you reopen a process. (35989) Previously, when a domain was not added to an Active Directory domain controller, the groups of that domain were synchronized. Now, the groups are not synchronized. Instead, you must add all used domains to the Active Directory domain controller manually. This supports adding multiple domains. (34000) For Forms installed on .NET 4.7 in Windows Core machines, you cannot save to repository as a TIFF. To resolve this issue, open a support case to install the correct library. (33843) When you import a form to a new server, themes and icons are lost. (27628) The new validation options in Forms 10.3 may not be backwards compatible with existing custom validation implementations. Single line, drop-down, date, time, email, number, and currency fields have a fixed height of 26px. (153515) You cannot use Chinese variable names when dynamically assign tasks to a team using a team filter. (153135) If Forms is not configured for HTTPS, you will receive a "Geolocation permissions denied" er

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.1. 10 years ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013761/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-10-1- Laserfiche 10, Laserfiche 10.1, Web Access 10, Web Access 10.1 Summary Laserfiche 10.1 ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013761/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche 10.1. Product: Laserfiche 10, Laserfiche 10.1, Web Access 10, Web Access 10.1 KB: 1013761 Published: April 26, 2016 Last Updated: April 28, 2016 Summary Laserfiche 10.1 introduces several new features that make it easier to work with documents in your repository. New Feature Overview In Web Access 10.1, you can now open both PDFs and Microsoft Office documents directly in the document viewer, without needing to download and open them locally. With this feature, you can view these document types in the same way you would an imaged document. Metadata and other information about the document is immediately available right in the details pane of the document viewer. Note: The Office Viewer requires that your site implement Office Web Apps Server. For more information, see General Services in the Web Access section of the Laserfiche Administration Guide. When importing images or a PDF file into Laserfiche using Web Access, you will now be provided with a preview of the images or PDF directly in the import dialog. This allows you to refer to the document if you need additional information to help you file the document or provide its metadata. When working with a document or folder, you may want to be able to quickly open an audit report for information on that entry. For instance, you might want to be able to see the history of who accessed or modified the document. Once you have configured the relevant audit reports in Audit Trail Reporter, you can launch them for a specific entry directly from within Web Access. (This feature respects the same security as Audit Trail Reporter: if a user does not have sufficient rights to run an audit report, the option to launch it will not be available.) You can now compare changes when viewing version information for Microsoft Office documents stored in Laserfiche, in both the Laserfiche Client and Web Access. When comparing changes, differences between the versions will be displayed using the native Microsoft Office revision markup, allowing you to quickly locate what was changed between two versions. This functionality is supported with Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, and PowerPoint presentations. Column profiles allow users to save sets of folder browser columns and then quickly apply them to view different information about entries. In Web Access 10.1, you can now apply column profiles to search results as well as to regular folders. You can now copy text directly from the images in Laserfiche documents in Web Access, allowing you to quickly populate metadata with copy and paste. Using the new Select Text command will retrieve the OCRed text from the selected portion of the document and copy it to the clipboard, allowing you to easily paste it anywhere you choose. Additional Changes The following minor changes and fixes were also added to Laserfiche 10.1. Note: Laserfiche 10.1 is a cumulative update and also includes all changes included in Update 1 and Update 2. Web Administration Console Fixed potential install issues on French operating systems. (138675) Desktop Administration Console Trusted subdomains now display when linking windows accounts to repository users in Laserfiche Rio (140446) Copying users now retains the original audit settings. (139320) An incorrect "Access denied." error message no longer appears when modifying list field values where the user has appropriate rights to the field being modified, but not to other fields in the template. (132037) Audit Trail Reporter Significantly improved log import performance in Oracle environments (85718) Fixed an issue where connections to the repository did not always close completely. (112773) Improved handling of daylight savings time when displaying the audit report. (142643) Desktop Laserfiche Client Calendar pickers now properly display in Hijri environments. (139120) The option to enable HTTP logging is no longer present for products and operating systems on which it is no longer relevant. (140279) Generating text on PDFs in the repository will first try to perform text extraction, and will OCR if text extraction fails. (140905) Custom tokens can now be added as properties for custom toolbar buttons. (142474) Pages can now be generated from PDF types that previously caused issues. (127250, 134238, 141372, 132734) Password protected PDFs now correctly follow the monochrome option during page generation. (139291) Using tokens in the folder path during document import no longer limits the path to 255 characters. (140140) The parent field token now resolves correctly in fields where the token value has more characters than the field length. (139859) File import tokens now correctly resolve during import on multi-value fields. (133060) An issue causing the Laserfiche Client to crash when multi-selecting and editing dynamic fields has been resolved. (140890) The preview pane will now be displayed when a folder is selected in the Folder Browser. (1

rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche 8.3.0.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for Laserfiche 8.3.0. Product: Laserfiche 8.3 KB: 1012849 Published: November 28, 2011 Last Updated: August 9, 2012 Summary Laserfiche 8.3.0 is a major release to the core Laserfiche Server and Client products that include a wide range of new features. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1012848. Installation Information Laserfiche 8.3 is available on the Support Site in two packages: Avante and Rio. Team and United customers should use the Avante 8.3 installation package. Rio 8.3 includes License Manager 8.2. License Manager 8.2 is fully compatible with Laserfiche 8.3 products. The installation process works best when run from local media. Certain aspects of the installation wizard may not function correctly when run directly from a network drive or file share. For example, the installation may not automatically resume following a reboot after installing the setup prerequisites; you must manually restart the installation. Or you may be unable to assign a domain account during the service configuration step of the installation. Laserfiche Server System Requirements Minimum Server Requirements CPU: 1.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1), Windows Server 2003 R2, Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 Database engine: MSDE (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2000 (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2005 Express Edition (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2005 (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2008, Microsoft SQL Server 2008 Express, Oracle 9i Release 2 (9.2.0.8+), Oracle 10g (10.2.0.4+), Oracle 10g Client (10.2.0.5+), Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+), Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+) Audit Trail Web Reporting Requirements Web Server: IIS 5.1 (Windows XP), IIS 6 (Windows XP x64 and Windows Server 2003), IIS 7 (Windows Vista and Windows Server 2008), or IIS 7.5 (Windows 7 and Windows Server 2008 R2) DBMS: Microsoft SQL Server 2005, Microsoft SQL Server 2008, Oracle 10g, or Oracle 11g Web Browser: Internet Explorer 7, Internet Explorer 8, Firefox 2, or Firefox 3 on the computer from which you will configure and generate reports. Laserfiche Client System Requirements Minimum requirements CPU: 700 MHz or faster processor Memory: 128 MB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1), Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008, Windows 7 Web browser: Internet Explorer 6.0 Minimum requirements for Laserfiche Scanning and Laserfiche PhotoDocs CPU: 1 GHz or faster processor Memory: 256 MB RAM Minimum requirements for doing OCR CPU: 2.4 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM Recommended requirements CPU: 1 GHz or faster processor Memory: 256 MB RAM or more Recommended requirements for Laserfiche Scanning and Laserfiche PhotoDocs CPU: 2.4 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM or more Recommended requirements for doing OCR CPU: Dual core 2.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM or more Known Issues Product Issue Server You cannot modify the definition of a field if that field exists in the version history of a document. Administration Console The checkbox for enabling audit log timestamps is always disabled. As a workaround, use the Web Administration Console to enable the option. (82363) Administration Console You cannot host the audit log file on a mapped drive. (82261) Administration Console Unregistering and reregistering a repository resets the location of the audit logs. (34062) Audit Trail Audit Trail may not be fully optimized for large data sets. (81573) Audit Trail Large legends can potentially obscure chart data. (34888) Client 1012878 An "Error 2753. The File ''LFSession.exe.1606D57C_7C31_40C1_8AC2_32C0B957420'' is not marked for installation" Occurs When Installing Import Agent 8.1. Client Laserfiche may not properly display certain images without the latest Windows Imaging Component updates. Please see the following Microsoft Knowledge Base article (62978): http://support.microsoft.com/kb/954708 Send to Laserfiche The CTRL + S shortcut does not properly support the Send to Laserfiche feature in PowerPoint 2003. Click the Save toolbar button instead. (82271) Snapshot When processing a batch of documents, Snapshot may display a "The group file could not be found." error message. Snapshot will skip the problem document and continue processing the next document in the batch. (82035) Web Administration Console The Arabic (Kuwait) Hijri date calendar is 1 day off. (77745) Related Links 1012848 List of Changes for Laserfiche 8.3.0. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: Release Notes For Laserfiche 8.3.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes For Laserfiche 8.3.1. Product: Laserfiche 8.3.1 KB: 1012943 Published: April 9, 2012 Last Updated: August 9, 2012 Summary Laserfiche 8.3.1 is a maintenance release to the Laserfiche Server and Client products. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1012942. Installation Information Laserfiche 8.3.1 is available on the Support Site in two packages: Avante and Rio. Team and United customers should use the Avante 8.3.1 installation package. Rio 8.3.1 includes License Manager 8.2. License Manager 8.2 is fully compatible with Laserfiche 8.3 products. The installation process works best when run from local media. Certain aspects of the installation wizard may not function correctly when run directly from a network drive or file share. For example, the installation may not automatically resume following a reboot after installing the setup prerequisites; you must manually restart the installation. Or you may be unable to assign a domain account during the service configuration step of the installation. Laserfiche Server System Requirements Minimum Server Requirements Processor Type (CPU): Pentium 4, AMD Opteron, AMD Athlon 64, or faster Memory: 2 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1), Windows Server 2003 R2, Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 Database engine: MSDE (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2000 (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2005 Express Edition (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2005 (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2008, Microsoft SQL Server 2008 Express, Microsoft SQL Server 2012, Oracle 9i Release 2 (9.2.0.8+), Oracle 10g (10.2.0.4+), Oracle 10g Client (10.2.0.5+), Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+), Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+) Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, make sure that Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 or later with the corresponding version of Oracle Data Access Components (ODAC) is installed on the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server. Audit Trail Web Reporting Requirements Web Server: IIS 5.1 (Windows XP), IIS 6 (Windows XP x64 and Windows Server 2003), IIS 7 (Windows Vista and Windows Server 2008), or IIS 7.5 (Windows 7 and Windows Server 2008 R2) DBMS: Microsoft SQL Server 2005, Microsoft SQL Server 2008, Oracle 10g, or Oracle 11g Web Browser: Internet Explorer 7, Internet Explorer 8, Firefox 2, or Firefox 3 on the computer from which you will configure and generate reports. Laserfiche Client System Requirements Minimum requirements Processor Type (CPU): Pentium 4, AMD Opteron, AMD Athlon 64, or faster Memory: 128 MB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1), Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008, Windows 7 Web browser: Internet Explorer 6.0 Minimum requirements for Laserfiche Scanning and Laserfiche PhotoDocs CPU: 1 GHz or faster processor Memory: 256 MB RAM Minimum requirements for doing OCR CPU: 2.4 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM Recommended requirements CPU: 1 GHz or faster processor Memory: 256 MB RAM or more Recommended requirements for Laserfiche Scanning and Laserfiche PhotoDocs CPU: 2.4 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM or more Recommended requirements for doing OCR CPU: Dual core 2.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM or more Known Issues Product Issue Server You cannot modify the definition of a field if that field exists in the version history of a document. Administration Console The checkbox for enabling audit log timestamps is always disabled. As a workaround, use the Web Administration Console to enable the option. (82363) Administration Console You cannot host the audit log file on a mapped drive. (82261) Administration Console Unregistering and reregistering a repository resets the location of the audit logs. (34062) Audit Trail Audit Trail may not be fully optimized for large data sets. (81573) Audit Trail Large legends can potentially obscure chart data. (34888) Client 1012878 An "Error 2753. The File ''LFSession.exe.1606D57C_7C31_40C1_8AC2_32C0B957420'' is not marked for installation" Occurs When Installing Import Agent 8.1. Client Laserfiche may not properly display certain images without the latest Windows Imaging Component updates. Please see the following Microsoft Knowledge Base article (62978): http://support.microsoft.com/kb/954708 Send to Laserfiche The CTRL + S shortcut does not properly support the Send to Laserfiche feature in PowerPoint 2003. Click the Save toolbar button instead. (82271) Snapshot When processing a batch of documents, Snapshot may display a "The group file could not be found." error message. Snapshot will skip the problem document and continue processing the next document in the batch. (82035) Web Administration Console The Arabic (Kuwait) Hijri date calendar is 1 day off. (77745) Related Links 1012942 List Of Changes For Laserfiche 8.3.1. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy

rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche 9.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for Laserfiche 9.2. Product: Laserfiche 9.2 KB: 1013527 Published: September 26, 2014 Last Updated: June 9, 2015 Summary Laserfiche 9.2.0 (Released September, 2014) is a maintenance release to the Laserfiche Server and Client products. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1013528. Service Pack and Hotfix Information 1013588 List of Changes for Laserfiche 9.2 Service Pack 1. Installation Information Laserfiche 9.2 is available on the Support Site in two packages: Avante and Rio. Team and United customers should use the Avante 9.2 installation package. Rio 9.2 includes Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2, a new license management server for Laserfiche Rio that replaces License Manager 8. See Knowledge Base article 1013536 for information on new features. The installation process works best when run from local media. Certain aspects of the installation wizard may not function correctly when run directly from a network drive or file share. For example, the installation may not automatically resume following a reboot after installing the setup prerequisites; you must manually restart the installation. Or you may be unable to assign a domain account during the service configuration step of the installation. Upgrading from Laserfiche 9.1.x Laserfiche 9.2 no longer supports SQL Server 2000. If you are currently using SQL Server 2000 to host a Laserfiche repository, you must move to a SQL Server 2005 or later database before upgrading Laserfiche Server to version 9.2. Upgrading from Laserfiche 9.0.x After installing Laserfiche Server 9.2, the Server will be able to automatically update the database for you. This update process will happen the first time the Laserfiche Server attempts to load a Laserfiche 9.0.x repository. The update process happens behind the scenes, and does not require any user input. It is performed in-place, and does not create a new database. When the update is complete, your repository will be compatible with the Laserfiche 9.2 Server. Note: With large databases, the automatic schema update may some time. Be aware that your laserfiche repository will be inaccessible during this process even though the Laserfiche Server service is running. Check the Windows Event Log to see when the process is complete. Important: This update occurs in-place and does not create a new database. Therefore, you should ensure that you have a working backup before installing the Laserfiche 9.2 Server or registering your Laserfiche 9.0/9.1 repository to it. Once you have finished installing Laserfiche Server 9.2, you will also need to update your search catalog. Upgrading from Laserfiche 8 If you have a Laserfiche repository of version 8.0.x through 8.3.x, you do not need to perform a full migration to upgrade to Laserfiche 9. Instead, once you have upgraded your Server installation to Laserfiche 9, the Server will be able to automatically update the database for you. This update process will happen the first time the Laserfiche Server 9 attempts to load a Laserfiche 8.0.x-8.3.x repository. The update process happens behind the scenes, and does not require any user input. It is performed in-place, and does not create a new database. When the update is complete, your repository will be compatible with the Laserfiche 9 Server. Important: This update occurs in-place and does not create a new database. Therefore, you should ensure that you have a working backup before installing the Laserfiche 9 Server or registering your Laserfiche 8 repository to it. Once you have finished installing Laserfiche Server 9, you will also need to update your search catalog. During the Laserfiche Server 9 installation process, you will be prompted to update your Laserfiche 8.x search catalog files. All other Laserfiche products, including the Laserfiche Client, Client components, and Web Access, will be updated in place when you install the 9.x versions of those applications. Laserfiche Server System Requirements Minimum Server Requirements Processor Type (CPU): Pentium 4, AMD Opteron, AMD Athlon 64, or more recent processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 3), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 R2, Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008 (Service Pack 2), Windows 7, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 8, Windows Server 2012, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 R2 Database engine: Microsoft SQL Server 2005 (Service Pack 4), Microsoft SQL Server 2008 (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2008 R2 (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2012 (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2014, Oracle 9i Release 2 (9.2.0.8+), Oracle 10g (10.2.0.5+), Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+), Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+), Oracle 12c Note: If you are using an Oracle 12c server, please install the Oracle 11.2.0.3 client on the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server. Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, make sure that Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 or

rows.title: Release Notes For Laserfiche 9.0.3.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes For Laserfiche 9.0.3. Product: Laserfiche 9.0.3 KB: 1013325 Published: October 7, 2013 Last Updated: October 8, 2013 Summary Laserfiche 9.0.3 is a maintenance release to the Laserfiche Server and Client products. For a more detailed listing of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1013326. Installation Information Laserfiche 9.0.3 is available on the Support Site in two packages: Avante and Rio. Team and United customers should use the Avante 9.0.3 installation package. Rio 9.0.3 includes License Manager 8.3.1. License Manager 8.3.1. is fully compatible with Laserfiche 9.0.3 products. The installation process works best when run from local media. Certain aspects of the installation wizard may not function correctly when run directly from a network drive or file share. For example, the installation may not automatically resume following a reboot after installing the setup prerequisites; you must manually restart the installation. Or you may be unable to assign a domain account during the service configuration step of the installation. Upgrading from Laserfiche 8 If you have a Laserfiche repository of version 8.0.x through 8.3.x, you do not need to perform a full migration to upgrade to Laserfiche 9. Instead, once you have upgraded your Server installation to Laserfiche 9, the Server will be able to automatically update the database for you. This update process will happen the first time the Laserfiche Server 9.0 attempts to load a Laserfiche 8.0.x-8.3.x repository. The update process happens behind the scenes, and does not require any user input. It is performed in-place, and does not create a new database. When the update is complete, your repository will be compatible with the Laserfiche 9 Server. Important: This update occurs in-place and does not create a new database. Therefore, you should ensure that you have a working backup before installing the Laserfiche 9 Server or registering your Laserfiche 8 repository to it. Once you have finished installing Laserfiche Server 9, you will also need to update your search catalog. During the Laserfiche Server 9 installation process, you will be prompted to update your Laserfiche 8.x search catalog files. All other Laserfiche products, including the Laserfiche Client, Client components, and Web Access, will be updated in place when you install the 9.x versions of those applications. Laserfiche Server System Requirements Minimum Server Requirements Processor Type (CPU): Pentium 4, AMD Opteron, AMD Athlon 64, or more recent processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 3), Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2003 R2, Windows Vista (Service Pack 2), Windows Server 2008 (Service Pack 2), Windows 7, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 8, Windows Server 2012 Database engine: MSDE (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2000 (Service Pack 3), Microsoft SQL Server 2005 (Service Pack 2), Microsoft SQL Server 2008, Microsoft SQL Server 2008 R2, Microsoft SQL Server 2012, Oracle 9i Release 2 (9.2.0.8+), Oracle 10g (10.2.0.5+), Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+), Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+), Oracle 12c Note: If you are using an Oracle 12c server, please install the Oracle 11.2.0.3 client on the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server. Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, make sure that Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 or later with the corresponding version of Oracle Data Access Components (ODAC) is installed on the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server. Note: Express editions of the above Microsoft SQL Server versions are supported. Audit Trail Web Reporting Requirements Web Server: IIS 5.1 (Windows XP), IIS 6 (Windows XP x64 and Windows Server 2003), IIS 7 (Windows Vista and Windows Server 2008), IIS 7.5 (Windows 7 and Windows Server 2008 R2), IIS 8 (Windows 8 and Windows Server 2012) DBMS: Microsoft SQL Server 2005, Microsoft SQL Server 2008 (R2), Microsoft SQL Server 2012, Oracle 10g, or Oracle 11g Web Browser: Internet Explorer 7 or later, Firefox 2 or later Laserfiche Web Administration Console Requirements Web Server: IIS 5.1 (Windows XP), IIS 6 (Windows XP x64 and Windows Server 2003), IIS 7 (Windows Vista and Windows Server 2008), IIS 7.5 (Windows 7 and Windows Server 2008 R2), IIS 8 (Windows 8 and Windows Server 2012) Web Browser: Internet Explorer 8 or later, Firefox 3 or later, Safari, Chrome Known Issues Laserfiche Server 9 is compatible with License Manager 8.2 or higher. (94414) When viewing a PDF in the Folder Browser's Document Preview Pane or the Document Viewer's Electronic File Pane, hyperlinks in the PDF do not work. (94419) Remote computers cannot view a shared Snapshot printer's Laserfiche Snapshot Properties dialog box. (92426) Customized Document Viewer toolbars may be duplicated when upgrading from Laserfiche 9.0 to Laserfiche 9.0.1. (96492) Language Pack Known Issues In the Custom Setup step of the installation wizard, component descriptions are in English. (96169) The Laserfiche S
